title: Technical Project Manager
descriptionHtml:
Company Overview:

Sundew is a leading digital transformation firm with a 18 year legacy of excellence. We specialize in digital strategy, application development, and engineering, utilizing MEAN, MERN, and LAMP stacks, with PHP Laravel as our primary proficiency. As we continue to expand, we seek a dynamic and experienced Technology Project Manager to lead our projects, drive technology decisions, and contribute to the growth of our organization.


Key Responsibilities:

  • Plan, execute, and deliver IT projects within scope, budget, and timeline.
  • Collaborate with developers, QA teams, system administrators, and other technical stakeholders to define project requirements and deliverables.
  • Create detailed project plans, schedules, and resource allocation strategies.
  • Facilitate Agile ceremonies (daily stand-ups, sprint planning, retrospectives) or Waterfall stage gates as per project requirements.
  • Identify risks, implement mitigation plans, and resolve project-related issues.
  • Coordinate with vendors, contractors, and cross-functional teams for smooth project execution.
  • Ensure adherence to IT governance, security standards, and compliance frameworks (ISO 27001, GDPR, SOC 2).
  • Oversee UAT (User Acceptance Testing) and deployment processes.
  • Drive process improvements to increase efficiency and delivery quality.
  • Maintain comprehensive project documentation for audit and knowledge sharing.


Required Skill Sets:

Education and Experience:

  • MCA/B.Tech or equivalent
  • 10+ years of overall hands-on coding experience
  • Min 5+ years of IT project management experience, preferably in custom application/product development from scratch.

Technical Proficiency:

  • Proven track record of successfully managing cross-functional IT projects.
  • Expertise in MEAN, MERN, and LAMP stacks.
  • Proficiency in PHP Laravel.

Communication Skills:

  • Excellent verbal and written communication skills.
  • Client-facing communication experience.

Agile and Project Management:

  • Experience with JIRA and Agile Development Methodologies (SCRUM), Waterfall
  • Proven ability to manage and lead project teams.

Account Management:

  • Demonstrated ability to grow business within client accounts.
  • Strong interpersonal skills and relationship-building capabilities.

Presentation and Data Skills:

  • Exceptional presentation skills.
  • Strong data analysis skills for decision-making.

Documentation:

  • Meticulous attention to detail in documentation.
  • Proficient in creating and maintaining project documentation.

title: Manager, Project/Program Management (P)
descriptionHtml:
At Alcon, we are driven by the meaningful work we do to help people see brilliantly. We innovate boldly, champion progress, and act with speed as the global leader in eye care. Here, you’ll be recognized for your commitment and contributions and see your career like never before. Together, we go above and beyond to make an impact in the lives of our patients and customer. We foster an inclusive culture and are looking for diverse, talented people to join Alcon. As an Organizational Project Coordinator, you will support Organizational Project Managers by providing strong execution support, coordination, and operational rigor across cross‑functional initiatives. This role is focused on enabling efficient project delivery rather than owning project decisions or outcomes.

In this role a typical day will include:

  • Supporting Organizational Project Managers in maintaining integrated project plans, schedules, milestones, and dependencies.
  • Tracking action items, risks, issues, and dependencies; following up with owners to ensure timely progress.
  • Coordinating inputs from cross‑functional contributors to support on‑time delivery of project deliverables.
  • Preparing clear, concise project status updates, dashboards, and summaries for PM and leadership review.
  • Maintaining project documentation, trackers, and artifacts in approved tools and repositories.
  • Capturing meeting notes, decisions, and actions; distributing materials and tracking follow‑through.
  • Providing day‑to‑day coordination support that enables Project Managers to focus on leadership, decision‑making, and stakeholder engagement.

Scope & Ways of Working

  • Owns: Coordination, tracking, documentation, meeting support, and operational follow‑through.
  • Supports: Project planning, status reporting, communication preparation, and portfolio visibility.
  • Does not own: Strategic decisions, scope changes, budget ownership, or delivery accountability (retained by the Organizational Project Manager).

WHAT YOU’LL BRING TO ALCON:

  • Bachelor’s Degree or Equivalent year of directly related experience (or high school + 13 yrs; Assoc. +9 yrs; M.S. +2 yrs; PHD +0 yrs)
  • The ability to fluently read, write, understand and communicate in English
  • 5 Years of Relevant Experience
  • Travel: None
  • Relocation: Yes

Preferred Qualifications

  • 4–7 years of experience in project coordination, PMO, or operational support roles.
  • Strong organizational skills with attention to detail and follow through.
  • Proficiency with project tracking tools, Excel, PowerPoint, and collaboration platforms.
  • Experience supporting cross functional teams in a regulated or matrixed environment preferred.
  • Formal project management, learning management or change management certification preferred (e.g., PMI, PROSCI, ATD)
  • Direct experience on internally facing initiatives with a focus on associate experience - such as process improvement, transformations, reorganizations, integrations, etc.
  • Experience within Medical Device, Pharmaceuticals or similar regulated industry

HOW YOU CAN THRIVE:

  • Collaborate with teammates to share standard processes and findings as work evolves.
  • See your career like never before with focused growth and development opportunities.
  • Join Alcon’s mission to provide outstanding, innovative products and solutions to improve sight, improve lives, and grow your career!
  • Alcon provides robust benefits package including health, life, retirement, flexible time off and much more.

Shift timings - 2 PM to 11 PM

ATTENTION: Current Alcon Employee/Contingent Worker

If you are currently an active employee/contingent worker at Alcon, please click the appropriate link below to apply on the Internal Career site.

Find Jobs for Employees

Find Jobs for Contingent Worker

Alcon is an Equal Opportunity Employer and takes pride in maintaining a diverse environment. We do not discriminate in recruitment, hiring, training, promotion or other employment practices for reasons of race, color, religion, gender, national origin, age, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, disability, or any other reason.

As the global leader in eye care with a heritage spanning over 75 years, we offer the broadest portfolio of products to enhance sight and improve people’s lives. Our Surgical and Vision Care products touch the lives of more than 260 million people in over 140 countries each year living with conditions like cataracts, glaucoma, retinal diseases and refractive errors. Our more than 25,000 associates are enhancing the quality of life through innovative products, partnerships with Eye Care Professionals and programs that advance access to quality eye care. Learn more at www.alcon.com.

title: Technical Project Manager
descriptionHtml:
About Dodo Payments

Dodo Payments is the billing and payments platform for AI-first companies. From credit-based billing and usage metering to subscriptions and global payments, we give AI and SaaS companies everything they need to monetize without building billing infrastructure. As a global Merchant of Record, we enable payments across 190+ countries, 80+ currencies, and 40+ payment methods. We're trusted by 25,000+ builders and founders worldwide and backed by leading global investors.

About The Role

We're looking for a Technical Project Manager to drive execution across our engineering, product, and design teams. You'll own project delivery end to end: scoping, planning, unblocking, and shipping. Payments infrastructure demands precision; you'll coordinate complex, cross-functional initiatives spanning billing systems, payment integrations, compliance, and developer tooling, and keep them on track in a fast-moving startup environment.

What You'll Do

  • Own planning and delivery of cross-functional technical projects from kickoff to launch
  • Translate product requirements into clear execution plans with milestones, owners, and timelines
  • Run sprint planning, standups, and retrospectives; keep engineering velocity high and visible
  • Identify risks, dependencies, and blockers early, and drive them to resolution
  • Coordinate launches involving engineering, product, design, compliance, and go-to-market teams
  • Manage external dependencies with payment partners, banks, and infrastructure vendors
  • Maintain clear, up-to-date project documentation and status reporting for leadership
  • Improve our development processes, tooling, and delivery workflows as we scale

What We're Looking For

  • 2-5 years of experience as a Technical Project Manager, Program Manager, or similar role in a software product company
  • Strong technical fluency: you can read API docs, follow architecture discussions, and hold your own with engineers
  • Experience managing projects in agile environments (Scrum/Kanban) with tools like Linear, Jira, or similar
  • Excellent written and verbal communication; you make complex status simple for any audience
  • Proven ability to manage multiple concurrent workstreams without dropping details
  • Bias for action and comfort with the ambiguity and pace of an early-stage startup

Nice to Have

  • Experience in fintech, payments, billing, or other regulated infrastructure products
  • Familiarity with developer-facing products (APIs, SDKs, webhooks)
  • Exposure to compliance-driven projects (PCI DSS, KYC, tax)
  • Engineering or computer science background

Why Join Us

  • Build the monetization layer for the AI economy at one of the fastest-shipping teams in fintech
  • High ownership and direct impact: your work ships and customers feel it
  • Work alongside a passionate, tight-knit team in our Bangalore office
  • Competitive salary and meaningful equity

Skills: compliance,linear,projects,teams,clickup,infrastructure,project management

title: Assistant Program Manager
descriptionHtml:
Assistant Manager – Program Management (EWIS)

Location: Bengaluru, Karnataka

Company: SASMOS HET Technologies Ltd.

About SASMOS

SASMOS HET Technologies is a global engineering and manufacturing company specializing in high-reliability interconnect, EWIS, fiber-optic, and photonics solutions for Aerospace, Defence, Space, Telecom, and Industrial applications. Serving leading OEMs across India, Europe, and the US, SASMOS is driven by engineering excellence, precision manufacturing, and a commitment to delivering mission-critical solutions to global customers.

Role Overview

We are looking for an experienced Assistant Manager – Program Management (EWIS) to support the successful execution and delivery of Electrical Wiring Interconnection System (EWIS) programs for Aerospace and Defence customers.

The incumbent will work closely with Engineering, Manufacturing, Supply Chain, Quality, Planning, and Operations teams to ensure programs are delivered on time, within budget, and in compliance with customer requirements. This role requires strong program management capabilities, stakeholder management skills, and experience in complex manufacturing environments.

Key Responsibilities

  • Manage program schedules, milestones, and customer deliverables for EWIS projects.
  • Coordinate with Engineering, Manufacturing, Quality, Supply Chain, and Planning teams to ensure seamless program execution.
  • Monitor material readiness, production progress, and delivery commitments.
  • Track program performance against timelines, quality requirements, and customer expectations.
  • Identify risks, bottlenecks, and delays, and drive mitigation plans.
  • Manage engineering changes, customer requirements, and configuration control activities.
  • Prepare and present program dashboards, status reports, and management reviews.
  • Support customer communications related to schedules, deliverables, and project updates.
  • Monitor program costs and support adherence to commercial commitments.
  • Ensure compliance with contractual obligations and internal quality processes.

Qualifications & Experience

  • Bachelor's Degree in Engineering (Electrical, Electronics, Mechanical, Aerospace, or related discipline).
  • 4–8 years of experience in Program Management, Project Management, Manufacturing Operations, or Customer Program Management.
  • Experience in EWIS, Wiring Harness, Cable Harness, Electro-Mechanical Assemblies, Aerospace, Defence, or high-reliability manufacturing environments preferred.
  • Strong understanding of project planning, scheduling, and execution methodologies.
  • Familiarity with ERP systems (SAP preferred).
  • Excellent communication, stakeholder management, and problem-solving skills.

Preferred Skills

  • Program Management
  • EWIS (Electrical Wiring Interconnection Systems)
  • Wiring Harness & Cable Harness Manufacturing
  • Aerospace & Defence Manufacturing
  • Customer Program Management
  • Project Planning & Scheduling
  • Risk Management
  • Supply Chain Coordination
  • SAP / ERP Systems
  • APQP, PFMEA & Quality Systems
  • Cross-Functional Team Management

Why Join SASMOS?

Join a rapidly growing global organization serving leading Aerospace, Defence, Space, and Industrial customers. At SASMOS, you will have the opportunity to work on mission-critical programs, collaborate with highly skilled teams, and contribute to cutting-edge engineering and manufacturing solutions.

Experience: 4–8 Years

Employment Type: Full-Time

Work Location: Bengaluru, Karnataka
descriptionText:
Assistant Manager – Program Management (EWIS)
Location: Bengaluru, Karnataka
Company: SASMOS HET Technologies Ltd.
About SASMOS
SASMOS HET Technologies is a global engineering and manufacturing company specializing in high-reliability interconnect, EWIS, fiber-optic, and photonics solutions for Aerospace, Defence, Space, Telecom, and Industrial applications. Serving leading OEMs across India, Europe, and the US, SASMOS is driven by engineering excellence, precision manufacturing, and a commitment to delivering mission-critical solutions to global customers.
Role Overview
We are looking for an experienced Assistant Manager – Program Management (EWIS) to support the successful execution and delivery of Electrical Wiring Interconnection System (EWIS) programs for Aerospace and Defence customers.
The incumbent will work closely with Engineering, Manufacturing, Supply Chain, Quality, Planning, and Operations teams to ensure programs are delivered on time, within budget, and in compliance with customer requirements. This role requires strong program management capabilities, stakeholder management skills, and experience in complex manufacturing environments.
Key Responsibilities

- Manage program schedules, milestones, and customer deliverables for EWIS projects.
- Coordinate with Engineering, Manufacturing, Quality, Supply Chain, and Planning teams to ensure seamless program execution.
- Monitor material readiness, production progress, and delivery commitments.
- Track program performance against timelines, quality requirements, and customer expectations.
- Identify risks, bottlenecks, and delays, and drive mitigation plans.
- Manage engineering changes, customer requirements, and configuration control activities.
- Prepare and present program dashboards, status reports, and management reviews.
- Support customer communications related to schedules, deliverables, and project updates.
- Monitor program costs and support adherence to commercial commitments.
- Ensure compliance with contractual obligations and internal quality processes.
Qualifications & Experience

- Bachelor's Degree in Engineering (Electrical, Electronics, Mechanical, Aerospace, or related discipline).
- 4–8 years of experience in Program Management, Project Management, Manufacturing Operations, or Customer Program Management.
- Experience in EWIS, Wiring Harness, Cable Harness, Electro-Mechanical Assemblies, Aerospace, Defence, or high-reliability manufacturing environments preferred.
- Strong understanding of project planning, scheduling, and execution methodologies.
- Familiarity with ERP systems (SAP preferred).
- Excellent communication, stakeholder management, and problem-solving skills.
Preferred Skills

- Program Management
- EWIS (Electrical Wiring Interconnection Systems)
- Wiring Harness & Cable Harness Manufacturing
- Aerospace & Defence Manufacturing
- Customer Program Management
- Project Planning & Scheduling
- Risk Management
- Supply Chain Coordination
- SAP / ERP Systems
- APQP, PFMEA & Quality Systems
- Cross-Functional Team Management
Why Join SASMOS?
Join a rapidly growing global organization serving leading Aerospace, Defence, Space, and Industrial customers. At SASMOS, you will have the opportunity to work on mission-critical programs, collaborate with highly skilled teams, and contribute to cutting-edge engineering and manufacturing solutions.

companyDescription:
SASMOS HET Technologies Limited is a leading manufacturer of wiring harness, Electro-Mechanical assemblies and Unit Integration products in the Aerospace, Defence and Marine industry sphere in India.  
SASMOS was started its operation in 2007 with a passion to serve and excel in the area of electrical interconnection solutions focusing on the niche and quality driven needs of Aerospace and Defence Industry. It is led by a group of technology professionals having vast experience in the field of precision engineering and interconnection technology meeting international expectation for more than a decade. This team, supported by Netherland based Lumipol, initiated business operations by taking over HET – a company engaged in interconnection technology serving aerospace and defence industries for more than three decades in India.
SASMOS has progressed from being a start-up organisation to a preferred partner by OEM's in the industry and created the benchmark in the Harness manufacturing industries in India. Currently SASMOS exports more than 70% of its products to Israel, US and Europe.
SASMOS is an AS9100 and ISO 9001 certified organisation with its operators certified to International standards for Wiring Harness Manufacturing (IPC-WHMA-620) and for Electronic assemblies (IPC-A-610).  SASMOS has been awarded by “ELCINA award” for  consecutively during 2014,  2015 and 2016.

title: Technical Program Manager / Scrum Master - Mastercard Developers
descriptionHtml:
Our Purpose

Mastercard powers economies and empowers people in 200+ countries and territories worldwide. Together with our customers, we’re helping build a sustainable economy where everyone can prosper. We support a wide range of digital payments choices, making transactions secure, simple, smart and accessible. Our technology and innovation, partnerships and networks combine to deliver a unique set of products and services that help people, businesses and governments realize their greatest potential.

Title And Summary

Technical Program Manager / Scrum Master - Mastercard Developers

Overview

Mastercard Developers is the single entry point for Mastercard partners to access a diverse range of payment, data, loyalty, and security APIs. Our team is responsible for delivering an experience that ensures developers can quickly find the right API, assess its technical capabilities, and integrate it into new and innovative solutions. The Mastercard Developers team is looking for a technical program manager/scrum master to lead the successful execution of projects and programs, driving scalability and enhancing customer value for our platform. The ideal candidate will have a strong technical background, excellent project management skills, and a passion for delivering innovative solutions in a fast-paced environment.

Role

As a Technical Program Manager/scrum Master, You Will

  • Be responsible for planning, execution, and delivery, from inception to completion, for an existing technology product or program through collaboration with an international cross functional team including product, engineering, QA, UX and infrastructure teams.
  • Develop and maintain comprehensive project plans, timelines, capacity and resource allocations.
  • Plan and lead agile ceremonies and drive continuous development within the team.
  • Proactively identify and manage technical risks and dependencies, ensuring successful execution of projects.
  • Operate between teams and architectures overseeing the gaps between teams, processes, and systems, while removing all blocking issues leading to a successful delivery.
  • Collaborate with senior leadership to align program goals with broader business objectives.
  • Drive effective team organization and communication. Report out progress to internal stakeholders.


All About You

  • Demonstrated experience breaking down problems, organizing work, planning sprints, and delivering technical programs in agile delivery models (scrum, Kanban, etc.).
  • Strong ability to pay attention to detail and accuracy in all elements of project management
  • Solid understand of software development processes and Agile practices.
  • Proven track record of data driven decision-making and applying continuous improvement methodologies across teams.
  • Ability to predict challenges and seek to proactively head-off obstacles.
  • Ability to adapt swiftly and efficiently when priorities shift.
  • Enthusiastic about prioritizing product delivery to the customer over exclusively delivering story points.
  • Excellent time management and organizational skills.
  • Strong communications skills - both verbal and written - ability to communicate critical points efficient across a wide range of audiences.
  • Demonstrated experience building relationships, partnering with and influencing dependent teams while fostering respect among colleagues across the organization.
  • Huge plus if you have expertise in working with API based products.


Corporate Security Responsibility

 All activities involving access to Mastercard assets, information, and networks comes with an inherent risk to the organization and, therefore, it is expected that every person working for, or on behalf of, Mastercard is responsible for information security and must:

  • Abide by Mastercard’s security policies and practices;
  • Ensure the confidentiality and integrity of the information being accessed;
  • Report any suspected information security violation or breach, and
  • Complete all periodic mandatory security trainings in accordance with Mastercard’s guidelines.

title: Technical Program Manager, Forms of Payment, Payment Platform
descriptionHtml:
Minimum qualifications:

  • Bachelor's degree in a technical field, or equivalent practical experience.
  • 2 years of experience in program management.

Preferred qualifications:

  • Bachelor's degree in computer science, engineering, management information systems, or a related field.
  • 2 years of experience managing software development projects, cross-functional collaboration, and stakeholder management.
  • Experience in commerce/payments/platform products.

About The Job

A problem isn’t truly solved until it’s solved for all. That’s why Googlers build products that help create opportunities for everyone, whether down the street or across the globe. As a Technical Program Manager at Google, you’ll use your technical expertise to lead complex, multi-disciplinary projects from start to finish. You’ll work with stakeholders to plan requirements, identify risks, manage project schedules, and communicate clearly with cross-functional partners across the company. You're equally comfortable explaining your team's analyses and recommendations to executives as you are discussing the technical tradeoffs in product development with engineers.

Whether it is paying online with Autofill, using tap and pay in stores, or using the Google Pay app, the Payments team at Google is focused on making payments simple, seamless, and secure. In addition to consumer payment technologies, the Payments team also powers the money movement between Google and its consumers and businesses.

Responsibilities

  • Collaborate with team members and stakeholders to understand or identify defined work problems and program goals, obtain prioritized deliverables, and discuss program impact.
  • Prioritize program goals, understand and translate other stakeholders’ needs into program goals and prioritized deliverables with minimal assistance, and contribute to decisions on prioritizing goals and deliverables.
  • Define the scope of projects and develop, execute, or manage project plans for supported programs.
  • Review key metrics pertaining to a program, monitor potential metric deviations, and define corrective actions for critical deviations.
  • Identify, communicate, and collaborate with stakeholders within one or more teams to drive impact and work toward mutual goals.


Google is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. See also Google's EEO Policy and EEO is the Law. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know by completing our Accommodations for Applicants form .

title: Senior Manager Project Management (Sterile)
descriptionHtml:
Job Purpose

To manage end-to-end project execution for Injectable and Ophthalmic products, ensuring timely development, technology transfer, regulatory filings, validation, and commercial launches through effective cross-functional coordination.


Key Responsibilities

  1. Lead project planning, execution, monitoring, closure, and change management activities.
  2. Coordinate with R&D, Manufacturing, QA, QC, Regulatory Affairs, Engineering, Supply Chain, and Commercial teams for successful project delivery.
  3. Drive technology transfer, scale-up, validation, and commercialization of products for US and other regulated markets.
  4. Ensure achievement of project milestones and maintain OTIF (On-Time-In-Full) performance above 95%.
  5. Monitor project timelines, identify risks, and implement mitigation plans to ensure timely execution.
  6. Conduct project review meetings and provide regular progress updates to senior management and customers.
  7. Manage external development, manufacturing, and technology transfer projects with partners, CMOs, and CDMOs.
  8. Establish and implement standardized project management processes, governance, and reporting systems.
  9. Ensure compliance with GMP requirements, regulatory commitments, quality standards, and customer agreements.
  10. Drive annual filing, validation, and commercialization targets aligned with business objectives.
  11. Build and develop a high-performing project management team and support organizational capability development.
  12. Escalate critical project and resource issues in a timely manner and drive resolution through cross-functional collaboration.

Qualification & Experience

  • B.Pharm / M.Pharm
  • 8–12 years of experience in Project Management, Technology Transfer, Product Development, or Operations in Injectable/Ophthalmic formulations.
  • Experience in managing projects for USFDA and other regulated markets.

Key Competencies

  • Project Management
  • Technology Transfer
  • Stakeholder Management
  • Risk Management
  • Regulatory & GMP Compliance
  • Leadership & Team Management
  • Communication & Problem Solving

title: Senior Scrum Master / Project Manager
descriptionHtml:
About Company

Papigen is a fast-growing global technology services company, delivering innovative digital solutions through deep industry experience and cutting-edge expertise. We specialize in technology transformation, enterprise modernization, and dynamic areas like Cloud, Big Data, Java, React, DevOps, and more. Our client-centric approach combines consulting, engineering, and data science to help businesses evolve and scale efficiently.

About The Role

We are looking for an experienced Scrum Master with 6–7 years of IT experience and strong hands-on expertise in Agile delivery and Azure DevOps (ADO). The role involves facilitating Scrum ceremonies, enabling team performance, and ensuring effective sprint execution and tracking using Azure DevOps.

Key Responsibilities

  • Facilitate Scrum ceremonies: Sprint Planning, Daily Stand-ups, Reviews, and Retrospectives
  • Coach teams on Scrum and Agile best practices
  • Remove impediments and manage risks and dependencies
  • Collaborate with Product Owners on backlog grooming and prioritization
  • Track sprint progress, velocity, and metrics using Azure DevOps
  • Maintain ADO boards, dashboards, and reports
  • Drive continuous improvement and delivery excellence

Required Skills

  • 6–7 years of overall IT experience
  • 4+ years as a Scrum Master
  • Strong experience with Azure DevOps Boards
  • Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills
  • Scrum / SAFe certification preferred

Preferred Certifications

  • PMP (Project Management Professional)
  • Certified Scrum Master (CSM) or SAFe Scrum Master (SSM)
  • Agile Certified Practitioner (PMI-ACP)

Skills: data governance policies,scrum master,scrum,compliance requirements,data quality initiatives,team management,enterprise data standards,project management,data governance,pmp,csm,risk mitigation,agile,leadership,agile project management

title: Senior Project Manager
descriptionHtml:
Grounded in a talent-centric mindset, Maker Lab is a marketing agency designed to build decentralised technology-led agency teams, rooted in collaboration with clients from the ground up. Maker Lab's cross-functional teams of multidisciplinary specialists are embedded into client teams across digital marketing competencies — from measurement & analytics to content production.

Role Summary:

The Senior Project Manager is a multifaceted project manager responsible for building and executing integrated marketing campaigns across digital touchpoints, including websites, CRM, direct response media, social, and PR. The role requires an innovative problem-solver capable of delivering high-pressure launch initiatives on time and within budget, aligned with strategic goals.

This role requires you to work in an APAC-friendly time zone, starting at 5:30 a.m. IST.



Role: 

  • Lead annual campaign calendars in partnership with regional stakeholders, ensuring a unified view of publishing moments across digital platforms.
  • Oversee end-to-end production from concept to launch, serving as the primary point of contact for cross-functional teams.
  • Define and manage comprehensive project timelines while proactively mitigating risks to ensure on-schedule delivery.
  • Ensure brand consistency across integrated campaigns and manage stakeholder reviews and external agency briefs.
  • Lead asset localisation across APAC, triaging feedback to balance local nuances with budget and schedule constraints.
  • Execute site-wide merchandising using CMS tools and develop regional playbooks to establish best practices and optimise workflows.
  • Own the hands-on build and publishing of digital content, collaborating with engineers to troubleshoot CMS-related issues.
  • Act as a Subject Matter Expert (SME) for merchandising tools, driving continuous tool improvements through collaboration with Martech and Product teams.
  • Analyse post-launch metrics and compile comprehensive reporting decks to inform future regional and global campaign strategies.


You:

  • BA/BS degree or equivalent experience
  • 6+ years of professional experience, ideally within the technology sector or at global multinational corporations
  • Robust project management expertise, demonstrating the capacity to oversee multiple concurrent workstreams in high-pressure, fast-paced environments.
  • Exceptional interpersonal and communication skills, with a proven ability to manage cross-border stakeholders across diverse regions, including Japan, India, Australia, the US, Taiwan, and Singapore.
  • Solid background in e-commerce marketing, with a comprehensive understanding of the digital landscape and user experience best practices.
  • Accomplished self-starter with extensive experience interfacing with and aligning multiple cross-functional teams.
  • Technical proficiency in AI and digital design tools (e.g., Figma, Photoshop, Gemini), or the aptitude to rapidly master new technologies with minimal oversight.
  • Experience collaborating with creative agencies to develop high-quality assets and compelling copy.
  • Proven track record of high performance and productivity; capable of delivering results with minimal supervision while maintaining rigorous attention to detail and problem-solving excellence.
  • Exceptional verbal and written English communication and presentation skills. 

Please note: due to the high volume of applications, only successful candidates will be contacted with a follow-up introduction email.  If you do not receive an email from Maker Lab, your application has been unsuccessful this time around. Look out for future roles! By submitting your application, you consent to the collection, use and disclosure of your data by the Maker Lab team for the purposes of your application. Where your application contains personal data of third parties, you confirm that you have obtained all necessary consents to provide the Maker Lab team with such personal data and that we may collect, use and/or disclose such data in accordance with the Personal Data Protection Act 2012.

- Robust project management expertise, demonstrating the capacity to oversee multiple concurrent workstreams in high-pressure, fast-paced environments.
- Exceptional interpersonal and communication skills, with a proven ability to manage cross-border stakeholders across diverse regions, including Japan, India, Australia, the US, Taiwan, and Singapore.
- Solid background in e-commerce marketing, with a comprehensive understanding of the digital landscape and user experience best practices.
- Accomplished self-starter with extensive experience interfacing with and aligning multiple cross-functional teams.
- Technical proficiency in AI and digital design tools (e.g., Figma, Photoshop, Gemini), or the aptitude to rapidly master new technologies with minimal oversight.
- Experience collaborating with creative agencies to develop high-quality assets and compelling copy.
- Proven track record of high performance and productivity; capable of delivering results with minimal supervision while maintaining rigorous attention to detail and problem-solving excellence.

- Exceptional verbal and written English communication and presentation skills. 

Please note: due to the high volume of applications, only successful candidates will be contacted with a follow-up introduction email.  If you do not receive an email from Maker Lab, your application has been unsuccessful this time around. Look out for future roles! By submitting your application, you consent to the collection, use and disclosure of your data by the Maker Lab team for the purposes of your application. Where your application contains personal data of third parties, you confirm that you have obtained all necessary consents to provide the Maker Lab team with such personal data and that we may collect, use and/or disclose such data in accordance with the Personal Data Protection Act 2012.

Collaborating with culture brands like YouTube, Netflix, Amazon, and Google, we embed cross-functional teams of multidisciplinary marketing specialists into our clients’ ecosystems to help drive business growth and marketing efficiencies. Our solutions span various marketing services and have expanded to include marketing strategy and creative production for digital and content marketing, data analytics, CRM, as well as influencer engagement and social media management.

Maker Lab is part of global independent marketing business Miroma Group, a group of industry leading marketing and communications agencies that provide services across the marketing mix for clients including; Adidas, Amazon, eBay, Netflix, Sony, Spotify, and YouTube.

title: Technical Project Manager
descriptionHtml:
Agile Technical Manager (12–14 Years)

Role Purpose

The Agile Technical Manager is responsible for planning, coordinating, and governing customer implementations.

The role ensures successful delivery by managing project schedules, dependencies, risks, resources, and stakeholder communications while working closely with Business Analysts, Implementation Architects, Configurators, QA teams, DevOps, Product Teams, and customer stakeholders.


Key Responsibilities

  • Develop and manage implementation plans, timelines, and delivery milestones.
  • Establish and manage sprint cadence, planning activities, and delivery governance.
  • Coordinate delivery activities across Business, Product, Architecture, Configuration, QA, DevOps, and Support teams.
  • Manage customer-specific configuration workstreams including Rules Configuration (FEEL – Friendly Enough Expression Language), n8n, Workflow Configuration, form.io Configuration, Integration Configuration, User Access Configuration, Document Configuration, and Data Migration activities.
  • Provide regular delivery updates to customers and internal stakeholders.
  • Track project scope, schedule, budget, and resource utilization.
  • Facilitate cross-functional collaboration to ensure timely resolution of blockers.
  • Support release planning, deployment coordination, and go-live readiness activities.
  • Ensure adherence to delivery processes, governance standards, and implementation of best practices.
  • Drive successful transition to support and hyper-care activities.


Required Skills & Experience

  • Bachelor's degree in engineering, Information Systems, Computer Science, Business, or related field.
  • 12–14 years of experience in project management, technical program management, or software implementation delivery.
  • Strong experience in the Property & Casualty (P&C) Insurance domain; Knowledge of Commercial Lines and Specialty Insurance, considered an advantage.
  • Strong understanding of Agile, Scrum, Kanban, and hybrid delivery methodologies
  • Good experience of workflow automation platforms, configurable enterprise applications, and implementation-driven delivery models.
  • Familiarity with BPMN-based workflow automation and business process orchestration platforms such as Camunda, Flowable, Appian, or similar technologies
  • Understanding of enterprise integrations, APIs, cloud-native architectures, data migration, and deployment processes.
  • Experience managing cloud-native, SaaS, or AI-enabled product implementations.
  • Strong project planning, dependency management, risk management, and stakeholder communication skills.
  • Experience using Jira, Confluence, Azure DevOps, or similar delivery management tools.
  • Experience working with cross-functional technical and business teams.
  • Excellent communication, leadership, and problem-solving skills.
  • Proven experience working with insurance carriers and clients across North America or Europe.


Desired Skills

  • Experience with Intelligent Automation, Workflow Automation, Business Process Management (BPM), AI-enabled solutions, or Document Processing platforms.
  • Experience working with workflow platforms, business rules engines, integration frameworks, low-code/no-code platforms, or intelligent automation solutions.
  • Scrum Master, PMI-ACP, PMP, PRINCE2, SAFe Agilist, or equivalent Agile/Project Management certification.

title: IT Project Manager
descriptionHtml:
Job Description

Plans, monitors and controls cross-divisional information technology projects. Works with global project teams and senior management to achieve project objectives. Monitors the project from initiation through delivery while ensuring consistency with SAS’ overall strategy, commitments and goals.

Primary Responsibilities

  • Manages deadline-driven, team-oriented projects. Responsible for attainment of overall project objectives and goals.
  • Establishes budget and delivery schedule, based on project requirements and in consultation with stakeholders and internal and external project team members.
  • Determines resource needs and coordinates requests for personnel, equipment and other resources.
  • Directs activities of project staff. May provide technical leadership as appropriate for projects.
  • Initiates and tracks projects. Maintains project documentation including updates to Project/Portfolio Management systems, plans, schedules and status reports.
  • Manages project scope and customer expectations.
  • Works with team to define project goals and implementation strategies.
  • Negotiates with customer about project issues related to risks, cost, time, and deliverables.
  • Communicates project status to stakeholders and management. Provides presentations on projects (e.g. kick-off, scope, deliverables and schedule) as requested.
  • Uses appropriate interpersonal styles and communication methods to work effectively with business partners (e.g. peers, functional partners, cross-sector partners,
  • matrixed partners, external vendors, etc.) to meet mutual goals and objectives.
  • Organizes and manages assigned projects considering department, division, and corporate priorities and goals.

Requirements

(Explain the technical areas/skills required for hiring)

Essential

  • Strong organization and Planning skills
  • Excellent written and verbal skills
  • Demonstrated ability to plan, organize and deliver projects
  • Experience with formal project management methodology
  • Ability to lead a project team
  • Working knowledge of the working environment which applies to the scope of projects to be managed (project characteristics may include development, infrastructure, analytics, global, 3 party, compliance, etc.)
  • Ability to conceptualize client needs and translate them into implementation strategies Resolves problems with minimal management assistance, escalates appropriately
  • Strong negotiation, facilitation, and presentation skills
  • Global business perspective and effective global team management.

Complexity

  • Works on problems of diverse scope where analysis of data requires evaluation of identifiable factors. Demonstrates good judgment in selecting methods and techniques for obtaining solutions. Networks with senior internal and external personnel in own area of expertise.

Problem Solving

  • A seasoned, experienced professional with a full understanding of area of specialization; resolves a wide range of issues in creative ways. This job is the fully qualified, career oriented, journey-level position.

Mandatory Technical Skills

  • Strong understanding of IT project management methodologies (Agile, Waterfall, Hybrid).
  • Experience managing enterprise IT projects in a global environment.
  • Proficiency with project management tools (e.g., MS Project, Planner, Jira, Azure DevOps).
  • Demonstrated knowledge of ITIL processes and ServiceNow.
  • Strong communication, stakeholder management, and leadership skills.
  • Ability to manage multiple high-visibility projects simultaneously.

Certification

  • PMP (Project Management Professional) and/or Certified ScrumMaster (CSM) certification is strongly preferred.

Total Years of Relevant Experience

  • 6-8 years of related project management experience.

Education Preference

Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field required.

Equivalent combinations of education, training, and relevant experience may be considered.

title: Senior Project Manager
descriptionHtml:
About Company

Papigen is a fast-growing global technology services company, delivering innovative digital solutions through deep industry experience and cutting-edge expertise. We specialize in technology transformation, enterprise modernization, and dynamic areas like Cloud, Big Data, Java, React, DevOps, and more. Our client-centric approach combines consulting, engineering, and data science to help businesses evolve and scale efficiently.

About The Role

We are looking for an experienced Senior Project Manager with 8+ years of IT experience and strong hands-on expertise in QA project delivery, Agile methodologies (Scrum/SAFe), stakeholder management, Agile delivery and Azure DevOps (ADO). The role involves leading QA delivery across applications, ensuring engagements are planned, executed, and delivered on scope, timeline, and quality, while acting as the primary interface with different teams.

Key Responsibilities

  • Lead end-to-end QA project delivery and overall engagement management across applications
  • Own planning, execution, stakeholder communication, and ensure delivery aligns with scope, timelines, and quality standards
  • Oversee test strategy, QA governance, and quality oversight across the project lifecycle
  • Manage resource allocation, capacity planning, and coordination of distributed/cross-functional teams
  • Drive risk identification, mitigation planning, and management of dependencies and issues
  • Monitor and report on SLA compliance, project status, metrics, and delivery health to stakeholders and leadership
  • Facilitate Agile ceremonies (Sprint Planning, Stand-ups, Reviews, Retrospectives) and coach teams on Scrum/SAFe best practices
  • Act as the primary point of contact and interface with different teams, ensuring alignment on priorities and expectations
  • Collaborate with Product Owners, QA leads, and delivery teams on backlog grooming, prioritization, and test planning
  • Drive continuous improvement initiatives to enhance delivery quality and efficiency

Required Skills

  • 8+ years of overall IT experience, with significant experience in QA project/program management
  • Proven experience leading QA delivery and governance for large, distributed engagements
  • Strong working knowledge of Agile methodologies (Scrum/SAFe)
  • Hands-on experience with project tracking and reporting tools (e.g., Azure DevOps Boards, JIRA)
  • Experience managing SLAs, risk registers, and stakeholder reporting
  • Excellent communication, coordination, and stakeholder management skills, including experience working with client-side teams.
  • Experience coordinating distributed/offshore teams
  • Scrum/SAFe certification preferred

Preferred Certifications

  • PMP (Project Management Professional)
  • Certified Scrum Master (CSM) or SAFe Scrum Master (SSM)
  • Agile Certified Practitioner (PMI-ACP)
  • ISTQB or other QA-related certifications (a plus)

title: Business Program Manager, Data Center Operations
descriptionHtml:
Minimum qualifications:

  • Bachelor's degree in a related field, or equivalent practical experience.
  • 2 years of experience in accounting and managing accounts payable and accounts receivable.
  • 2 years of experience managing the financial lifecycle, including accounting, contracting, budgeting, and financial administration.

Preferred qualifications:

  • Experience with enterprise resource planning (ERP) software.
  • Experience in vendor management.
  • Experience in SAP, Sage, Oracle suite, or equivalent.
  • Knowledge of local tax and administration.
  • Ability to demonstrate strong financial administration and modeling skills, including performing extensive spreadsheet work and monthly variance reviews.
  • Excellent communication and presentation skills.

About The Job

As a Data Center Business Program Manager, you will support our facilities technicians, managing the maintenance agreements of all facility related systems. You will lead cost control and contractor management for maintenance tasks and small projects, and work closely with the site Environment, Health and Safety department to ensure safety operations. You will have a pivotal role in ensuring that contractors deliver quality services according to agreed key performance indicators and that safety measures are being performed.

The Data Center team designs and operates some of the most sophisticated electrical and HVAC systems in the world. We are an upbeat, creative, team-oriented group of engineers committed to building and operating powerful data centers.

Responsibilities

  • Plan and execute work assignments with varying degrees of supervision.
  • Act as the point of contact for projects as assigned by the facility manager.
  • Prepare a statement of work (SoW) for facility operations agreements.
  • Obtain facilities-related vendor proposals, prepare purchase orders, and review/approve invoices.
  • Work with support service vendors to resolve conflicts and issues under the direction of the facility manager.


Google is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. See also Google's EEO Policy and EEO is the Law. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know by completing our Accommodations for Applicants form .

If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know by completing our Accommodations for Applicants form .

title: Technical Project Manager
descriptionHtml:
Onebridge, a Marlabs Company, is a global AI and Data Analytics Consulting Firm that empowers organizations worldwide to drive better outcomes through data and technology. Since 2005, we have partnered with some of the largest healthcare, life sciences, financial services, and government entities across the globe. We have an exciting opportunity for a highly skilled Technical Project Manager to join our innovative and dynamic team.


Employment Type: Full Time or Contract

Location: India

Industry: IT & Services


Technical Project Manager | About You

As a Technical Project Manager, you are responsible for leading cross‑functional technology initiatives from concept through delivery, ensuring projects are well‑planned, well‑executed, and aligned with business objectives. You thrive at the intersection of business and technology, translating complex technical requirements into clear, structured project plans that teams can execute with confidence. You are highly organized, comfortable managing multiple workstreams simultaneously, and confident working with engineers, analysts, architects, and business stakeholders across the organization. Clear communication, proactive risk management, and a strong delivery mindset define your approach to driving projects forward in dynamic environments. You bring structure to ambiguity, anticipate challenges before they arise, and keep teams focused on delivering measurable, high‑quality results.


Technical Project Manager | Day to Day

  • Lead end‑to‑end planning and execution of technical projects across data, application, infrastructure, and platform initiatives.
  • Partner closely with engineering, product, and business teams to define project scope, timelines, dependencies, resource needs, and success criteria.
  • Develop, maintain, and communicate detailed project plans, status reports, and risk and issue logs throughout the project lifecycle.
  • Facilitate stand‑ups, planning sessions, retrospectives, and regular stakeholder updates to ensure alignment and transparency.
  • Identify and mitigate risks, resolve blockers, and manage change requests while maintaining project momentum and delivery commitments.
  • Ensure project deliverables meet defined quality standards and align with technical, security, and business requirements.


Technical Project Manager | Skills & Experience

  • 5+ years of experience in project or program management within a technical or technology‑driven environment.
  • Demonstrated experience leading complex technical projects using formal project management methodologies.
  • Strong understanding of the software development lifecycle and modern delivery practices.
  • Experience working within Agile, Scrum, Waterfall, or hybrid delivery models depending on project needs.
  • Proficiency with project management tools such as Jira, Confluence, MS Project, Smartsheet, or similar platforms.
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to effectively engage both technical and non‑technical stakeholders.
  • Proven ability to work independently, manage competing priorities, and drive projects to successful completion.

companySlogan: Decision Intelligence Delivered
companyDescription:
Onebridge, a Marlabs Company, is data and AI consulting firm that strives to improve outcomes for the people we serve through data and technology. We want to make you heroes in your organization!

We're a full-lifecycle data consulting firm. We've served some of the largest healthcare, life sciences, manufacturing, financial services, and government entities in the U.S. since 2005. Onebridge has been a top “Best Places to Work” in Indianapolis since 2015.

In 2024, Onebridge was acquired by Marlabs LLC, a global digital solutions company.

We are the go-to partner for Strategy, Execution, Enablement (SEE) across Strategic Data Management, Integration and Architecture, Business Intelligence (BI) & Analytics, and Enterprise Application Development.

Better data leads to better decisions, and better decisions lead to better outcomes. This is decision intelligence, and we're the right partner to help you get there. That's why our clients are so loyal!
